title: holarrhena procumbens
intro: Holarrhena procumbens, also known as the Indian arrowroot or Kola nut, is a plant species native to tropical Africa and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Holarrhena procumbens include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of digestive problems, such as diarrhea and dysentery. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of roots, leaves, or bark, and can be consumed as a decoction, powder, or infused in teas, highlighting its potential as a natural remedy for various ailments.
